# Feature: proof-jest

> Scope: scripts/proof/jest_purlin.js

## What it does

Jest custom reporter that parses proof markers from test titles matching `[proof:feature:PROOF-N:RULE-N:tier]` and emits feature-scoped proof JSON files alongside the corresponding spec files.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Test titles containing `[proof:<feature>:<PROOF-N>:<RULE-N>]` are parsed as proof markers; tier is optional and defaults to "default"
- RULE-2: Each proof entry records feature, id, rule, test_file (relative to rootDir), test_name, status (pass if "passed", fail otherwise), and tier
- RULE-3: Proof files are written to `specs/<feature_dir>/<feature>.proofs-<tier>.json`, where feature_dir is discovered by scanning `specs/**/*.md`
- RULE-4: When a feature has no matching spec file, proofs default to the `specs/` directory
- RULE-5: On run complete, old proof entries for the same feature are purged before writing new ones

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): A test titled `"does thing [proof:my_feat:PROOF-1:RULE-1]"` produces a proof entry for feature `my_feat`
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): The emitted proof JSON entry contains all required fields: feature, id, rule, test_file, test_name, status, tier
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Proof file is written next to the matching spec file in the correct directory
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): A proof for an unknown feature writes to `specs/<feature>.proofs-default.json`
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Running tests twice replaces old entries for the feature, not appending duplicates
